schema.appDescription: Calculate NSC maturity value for 5-year NSC at current rate of 7.7% p.a. Compounded annually, qualifies for Section 80C deduction.

howToSteps.name: Enter your details
howToSteps.text: Fill in the input fields for the NSC Calculator. All inputs have smart defaults.

howToSteps.name: View instant results
howToSteps.text: Results update in real time as you type — no submit button needed.

howToSteps.name: Explore breakdown
howToSteps.text: Open the year-by-year breakdown table to see how your numbers change over time.

howToSteps.name: Compare scenarios
howToSteps.text: Adjust the inputs to compare different scenarios and find the best option.

faq.q: What is NSC and what is its current interest rate?
faq.a: NSC (National Savings Certificate) is a post office saving scheme with a 5-year fixed tenure. Current rate for Q1 FY 2026-27 is 7.7% p.a. compounded annually. Available at post offices and select banks. Qualifies for Section 80C deduction.

NSC interest is taxable but treated uniquely — interest for years 1–4 is deemed reinvested and qualifies for 80C deduction each year (within the ₹1.5 lakh limit). Only the final year's interest (not reinvested) is taxable in year 5.

faq.q: Can NSC be used as loan collateral?
faq.a: Yes. NSC certificates can be pledged with banks and NBFCs as loan collateral. Banks typically offer 85–90% loan-to-value on NSC. They can be transferred to another person after 1 year on payment of a small fee.
Yes, the NSC Calculator on CalcPhi is completely free with no registration required.
No. All calculations run in your browser. We do not store any financial data.

faq.q: Can I use the NSC Calculator on mobile?
faq.a: Yes. CalcPhi is fully mobile-optimised and works on all modern smartphones.

examples.heading: ₹1 lakh NSC at 7.7% for 5 years
examples.text: ₹1 lakh invested in NSC at 7.7% p.a. grows to ₹1,44,903 at maturity. Interest earned: ₹44,903. Annual interest accruals (years 1–4) are reinvested and qualify for 80C deduction, reducing the effective tax burden significantly.

examples.heading: NSC vs PPF — 5-year comparison
examples.text: ₹1 lakh in NSC (7.7%) = ₹1,44,903 at 5 years. ₹1 lakh in PPF (7.1%) = ₹1,41,478. NSC gives higher nominal return but PPF interest is fully tax-free while NSC interest is taxable. NSC wins if you're in the 5% or nil tax bracket.
